skill.name: nature-reviewer
skill.description: Simulate a Nature-style reviewer assessment from the referee perspective rather than an author rebuttal. Use when the user wants a pre-submission review, reviewer report, peer-review style critique, novelty/significance/technical soundness assessment, reviewer-style manuscript evaluation, 审稿人视角评估, 预审稿意见, or Nature reviewer report. Return 3 reviewer reports plus a cross-review synthesis, grounded only in the local Nature reviewer source basis. Also trigger on general pre-submission review requests during academic writing even without the word "Nature", such as getting a mock peer review for any journal, critiquing a draft as a reviewer would, assessing novelty/rigor before submission, and Chinese phrasings like 审稿人视角、模拟审稿、预审、帮我审一下论文、投稿前自审、审稿意见模拟、找论文问题.
skill.summary: Simulates a Nature-style peer reviewer assessment from the referee side, producing 3 reviewer reports and a cross-review synthesis grounded in Nature's editorial criteria.
skill.author: Yuan1z0825

security_audit.summary: This is a pure text-based academic writing skill that simulates Nature-style peer review. All 176 'external_commands' static findings are false positives caused by markdown inline code formatting (backticks used to label section names and identifiers like `Reviewer 1`, `Cross-review synthesis`, `originality`, etc.) — no shell or Ruby execution exists. The 10 'weak cryptographic algorithm' hits are false matches on fragments of academic prose in the editorial criteria document. The 4 'system reconnaissance' findings reference manuscript reading, not system probing. The single 'hardcoded URL' is a Nature editorial policy URL in the README. No executable code, scripts, network calls, or prompt injection attempts were found.

content.user_title: Simulate Nature-style peer review before submission
content.value_statement: Researchers struggle to anticipate reviewer criticism before submitting to top journals like Nature. This skill reads your manuscript draft and produces a grounded, referee-perspective review package so you can strengthen your paper before submission.
actual_capabilities: Reads manuscript drafts, abstracts, sections, figure legends, or author notes as reviewer input
Evaluates manuscripts against Nature's five source-grounded axes: originality, scientific importance, interdisciplinary readership, technical soundness, and readability for nonspecialists
Produces exactly 3 reviewer reports that differ only in emphasis, never inventing identities or specialties
Generates a cross-review synthesis capturing consensus strengths, risks, and emphasis differences
Identifies who would be interested in the results and the technical failings blocking the authors' case
Flags unsupported claims and unassessable items with explicit boundary markers like AUTHOR_INPUT_NEEDED
limitations: Grounded only in the local editorial criteria source — cannot generalize to other journals' specific policies
Cannot make or claim the editor's final acceptance decision
Inventor reviewers differ only in emphasis; cannot simulate field-specific specialist critique beyond what source supports
Does not write author rebuttals — route to a rebuttal skill for that task

use_cases.title: Pre-submission Nature manuscript check
use_cases.description: A researcher submits a full draft to stress-test it against Nature's editorial criteria before sending it to the journal.
use_cases.target_user: PhD students and postdocs preparing a Nature submission

use_cases.title: Mock peer review for any journal
use_cases.description: An author wants a bounded, reviewer-style critique of a manuscript draft to identify weaknesses before formal submission.

use_cases.title: Broad-interest narrative testing
use_cases.description: A researcher wants to check whether their manuscript communicates significance to a non-specialist interdisciplinary audience.
use_cases.target_user: PIs and writing advisors evaluating manuscript framing

prompt_templates.title: Full draft review
prompt_templates.prompt: Please review the following manuscript draft from the Nature reviewer perspective. Evaluate it against the five source-grounded axes (originality, scientific importance, interdisciplinary readership, technical soundness, readability for nonspecialists). Return 3 reviewer reports plus a cross-review synthesis.
prompt_templates.scenario: Complete manuscript PDF or text paste

prompt_templates.title: Abstract-only quick check
prompt_templates.prompt: Here is my abstract and main figures. Assess the novelty and broad-interest potential from a Nature reviewer standpoint. What are the likely major concerns if this were sent to peer review?
prompt_templates.scenario: Early-stage manuscript with only abstract available

prompt_templates.title: Targeted technical soundness review
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the methods and results sections of this manuscript. Identify technical failings that must be addressed before the authors' case for significance is established. Do not comment on editorial decision.

prompt_templates.title: Broad-interest readability audit
prompt_templates.prompt: Evaluate whether this manuscript is readable for a nonspecialist interdisciplinary reader. Identify sections where background context is missing or where field-specific jargon blocks comprehension.

output_examples.input: A 4-page manuscript excerpt on a new CRISPR variant with claimed broad therapeutic implications.
output: Review setup: Input scope = methods + results excerpts. Boundary = full figures not provided.
Reviewer 1: Strong technical soundness in off-target analysis. Major concern: therapeutic breadth claim unsupported by in-vivo data shown.
Reviewer 2: Originality is moderate — incremental over existing base-editing tools. Broad-interest case needs stronger non-specialist framing.
Reviewer 3: Novel variant characterization is solid, but the interdisciplinary readership claim requires a schematic summary paragraph for non-specialists.
Cross-review synthesis: Consensus technical risk is the gap between in-vitro efficiency claims and absent in-vivo validation. Broad-interest case is undersold without a plain-language summary.

output_examples.input: An abstract claiming a new machine learning method that 'revolutionizes' protein structure prediction.
output: Review setup: Input scope = abstract only. Assessment boundary = methods and benchmarks not provided.
Reviewer 1: Not assessable from provided material — benchmark comparison vs. AlphaFold-class baselines is absent.
Reviewer 2: The word 'revolutionizes' is unsupported; the abstract provides no quantitative improvement claim.
Reviewer 3: Interdisciplinary readership potential is high, but technical failings block the significance claim until benchmark data is shown.
Risk / unsupported claims: AUTHOR_INPUT_NEEDED — full methods section and benchmark results required before significance can be evaluated.

best_practices: Provide the full manuscript draft or as many complete sections as possible for the strongest review output
Clearly state if the manuscript is partial so the skill can mark assessment boundaries instead of guessing
Use the output to identify which technical failings to fix before submission, not as a final editorial verdict
Review all three reports for emphasis differences to spot blind spots in your own framing
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill to draft author rebuttals or response letters — route to a rebuttal skill instead
Do not treat the reviewer reports as an editorial decision or accept/reject signal
Do not ask the skill to invent specific reviewer identities, institutions, or specialist biographies
Do not expect journal-policy certainty beyond what is in the local editorial criteria source

faq.question: Does this skill make the editor's accept/reject decision?
faq.answer: No. The skill explicitly avoids pretending to make the editor's decision. It produces referee-perspective feedback only.

faq.question: Can it review manuscripts for journals other than Nature?
faq.answer: It is grounded only in Nature's editorial criteria. For other journals, the criteria axes may not transfer directly.

faq.question: Will the three reviewers be different people?
faq.answer: No. The three reviewers share the same fact base and differ only in emphasis. The skill does not invent identities, specialties, or institutions.

faq.question: Can it write the author rebuttal for me?
faq.answer: No. This skill is for reviewer-style assessment only. Use a rebuttal-drafting skill for response letters.

faq.question: What happens if I only provide an abstract?
faq.answer: The skill will perform a bounded review and explicitly mark items as AUTHOR_INPUT_NEEDED or not assessable from the provided material.

faq.question: Does this skill send my manuscript anywhere?
faq.answer: No. It is a text-based review skill that operates only on the content you provide in the conversation.
